content.text:
Research Methodology
Semester:
3  |
Type:
UET3  |
VHS:
21h  |
Weekly hours:
1h30  |
Lecture:
1h30
Coefficient:
1  |
Credits:
2
Teaching Objectives
This course aims to teach students the skills and knowledge necessary to conduct effective scientific research. It covers the key stages of research, from formulating the topic to writing the final report, including literature review, research design, data collection and analysis, and oral presentation of results.
Prerequisites:
Scientific writing, language proficiency.
Course Content (21h)
1. Introduction to Scientific Research
Definition and objectives of scientific research
Types of research (fundamental, applied, etc.)
Role of methodology in research
Importance of mastering the language used
Research ethics in computer science: plagiarism, self-plagiarism, generative AI (ChatGPT, Deepseek, etc.)
2. Research Topic Formulation
Identifying and selecting a relevant research topic
Literature review: finding relevant sources, critical analysis, identifying gaps in the state of the art
Problem definition: formulating a clear research question, defining research hypotheses
Research objectives: defining specific objectives and expected results
3. Research Design
Choosing appropriate research methods (experimental, qualitative, quantitative, etc.)
Planning data collection (sampling, collection tools, etc.)
Developing a research plan
4. Data Collection
Implementing the research plan
Using data collection tools and techniques specific to computer science
Managing and organising collected data
5. Data Analysis
Using data analysis software
Processing and analysing collected data
Using statistical methods or other appropriate analysis techniques
Interpreting results and discussing implications
6. Writing the Research Report
Publication modes and structures: article, patent, thesis, book, poster, oral communication...
Writing software: LaTeX, Overleaf, etc.
Structure and organisation of a scientific research report
Clear and concise writing of different report sections
Presenting results and discussing conclusions
Bibliography management tools (APA, IEEE, Zotero, EndNote, etc.) and appendices
7. Oral Presentation of Results
Preparing and delivering an oral presentation of research results
Mastering scientific communication
Assessment
Final examination (in person): 100%
Bibliography
Beaud, Michel (2020).
L'art de la thèse
. La Découverte.
Stefan Kottwitz (2021).
LaTeX Beginner's Guide
. 2nd Edition, Packt Publishing.
Jean-Marie Dubois (2005).
La rédaction scientifique : mémoires et thèses
. Estem.
Michèle Lenoble-Pinson (1996).
La rédaction scientifique : conception, rédaction, présentation
. De Boeck Université.
Christine Gérard, Jean Germain (1985).
Recherche bibliographique et documentaire : généralités
. Faculté de philosophie et Lettres.
